title: Level 356
wikitext:
{{Transparent Theme 2}}{{Blurbackground|file=356BT-4.jpg|opacity=20%}} {{Rewrite in Progress |reason=This page has several issues which would warrant a rewrite. The first error is the concept of the page, which is can be simplified down to a children's library. There is no anomaly present — besides the presence of Facelings — so is there truly a point for this to be in the Backrooms? Next point is the subpar writing. This is present with the common checklist writing, and how many sentences feel more of a ramble than a coherent statement. For example: "The main area is the only section within all of level 356 to possess an exit door, which distinguishes itself from many other sections throughout the level, though, the exit doors are considered useless, as attempts to open it have simply failed." Besides the obvious verbosity, it is quite unclear what an exit door is. Since there is no universal exit door, we don't know wether this is a fire exit or just a normal door used for both entering and leaving. Why would it be called an exit door if you cannot leave it? And this area is already distinct enough, a food court is very different from a library. Many paragraphs have statements which have a style like what I showed. The first paragraph is the biggest example of this, it goes from explaining the appearance to explaining books, to explaining a filler day/night cycle. The entiy section also shows this, with the information it lays down all goes down to "Adult facelings work, child facelings read." This verbosity and bloat make the page boring to read. |author=TDGC GD |rewriter=14 De Julho |progress=16% QmQ[[File:Birmingham-brittish.gif]] }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 356: "Bright Tertiaries"}} {{Class 1|color-h=170|hexagon=1}} == '''Description''' == {{Blurimage|file=356BT-1.png|caption=Bright Tertiaries.}} Level 356, colloquially referred to as 'Bright Tertiaries', is portrayed as a minimalist, modern kids' library usually containing many overstimulating vibrant colors such as lime, cyan, and orange. The level contains mainly glossy and shiny surfaces, which are exceptionally polished with any mess, marking, or effects put onto the surfaces being erased or modified back to their original state by something or someone unknown. Books inside Level 356 feature words from many different languages, with some being on the same pages; there have been documentations of unknown languages in books. Holding any selected book can cause numbness in your hands, weakening the senses of the hands and arms, causing your sense of touch to dwindle until affected people have exited this level or wait a long period of time. Many windows usually show a lively grass field with trees, normally paired with a cityscape, although the outdoor sight is proven to be simply a picture, many wanderers feel a sense of serenity when looking into the outside greenery. The cycle between day and night is quite abnormal, with the lights in the level dimming down to a deep maroon hue, signifying night. Only then does the light revert to its white glow, signifying day. Any attempts to find the time length of day and night have failed due to machinery malfunction and inconsistent cycles. The pictures of the outdoor areas do not change during the day and night cycle. {{Blurimage|file=356BT??.jpg|caption=A photo of the main area showing other sections.}} The main area is the area where wanderers first spawn when entering this level. The main area section is weirdly the only parts of the level that does not get affected by the day and night cycle effects, instead keeping its same white hue throughout the day. The main area is the only section within all of Level 356 to possess an exit door, which distinguishes itself from many other sections throughout the level, though the exit doors are considered useless, as attempts to open them have simply failed. The main area is one of the more spacious sections in Level 356, some roofs can be lowered down to average human size. {{Blurimage|file=356BT-2.jpg|caption=One of the many explored sections within level 356 in the daytime.}} Many sections in level 356 vary in size and furniture, usually having less furnishings inside than main areas. The danger within rooms varies depending on whether it is day or night, with rooms in the daylight safer than those in night. Sections in level 356 are connected through hallways leading from the main area no longer than 4 feet. The map of the level cannot be graphed easily, as there are many rooms that constantly change in size and position throughout the level, though the main area is an exception, appearing the same to many wanderers' experiences. Sections in level 356 feature less elements containing color, with most walls and furniture being commonly a white color. Food courts occasionally lay abandoned and desolate in the cafeteria sections of level 356, with some adult facelings working in some stands (See entity section for more detail). While some food courts do not contain anything necessarily useful, others have edible food and drinks for people to consume. If any being manages to consume many of the same foods at once, they will noticeably be weaker, experiencing hallucinations, blackouts, and, without proper treatment, death. Inside food courts, are usually clean interiors, with faceling janitors seen to polish the inside, even so, some food stands are left unclean owing to the absence of janitor facelings. Due to the abundance of the three overstimulating colors,Cyan, Lime, and Orange. many wanderers that have traveled to this level have experienced eye strain and discomfort when staring at the hues and lights for a long time. Lights in the level give out lighter than required to, due to this, lights often blind wanderers, however effects are temporary with no evidence of people experiencing permanent blindness. If one exits the level with any form of eye pain, the pain may vanish at an instant. {{Blurimage|file=356BT-4.jpg|caption=A detailed section}}There are sections in level 356 in which there has been a unanimous discussion to call it detailed sections. Unlike many other explored sections, detailed sections are sections with more ornamental decoration, with many miniature details and usually wooden structures. This area includes less vibrant colors and the three main brilliant colors being noticeably desaturated and muted, if one ever enters the detailed section with any form of eye pain, they shall experience their eyes cured of any pain and effects, as colors in this section are slightly desaturated, please note that this will not happen every time wanderers enter this section. Books in this section, unlike others, include no imagery and text, with many blank books. Weirdly, all books in the detailed section are all roughly the same size. Despite the fact that many detailed sections include a second story people can access through stairs, all rooms on the floor lead to a blank cyan room. =='''Entities''' == The only singular entity that seemingly lives in this level are facelings. Adult facelings typically work as library workers, the adult facelings do not acknowledge your existence unless bothered by wanderers, or if a customer tries to buy a book by bringing any chosen book towards the adult faceling. Adult facelings are also seen roaming around the level at nighttime during their time off work, if an adult faceling ever finds a child faceling attacking a wanderer, they are known to calm the child faceling down. Adult facelings have a chance to work at food courts, if one tries to buy food from an adult faceling worker, the food will simply be free of charge. There are some adult facelings who work as janitor roles in food courts, polishing the cafeteria for a seemingly perpetual amount of time, if one tries to interfere with the janitor facelings, the janitors will simply push those away, forcefully if they have to. Child facelings live in the level along with adult facelings. Most child facelings are seen to sit on furniture to read books from the bookshelves. Strangely, child facelings who read books act different as regular child facelings normally do, being calmer than ones who wander. Should one find a child faceling who is wandering around the library alone in the nighttime, they are expected to keep calm and sprint away from the child faceling, as wandering child facelings are known to attack any being they come across that bothers them (Other than faceling entities) harming victims, though not to the point of death. If anyone is being attacked by a child faceling, they are expected to not fight back, fighting back will only cause the faceling to attack for a longer period of time, instead, the best option possible is to accept the attack, this action will calm the child facelings down quicker. The same child faceling will not fight anyone twice, as they remember others' face. Slamming a book on the ground can cause child facelings to get startled, luckily this will not alarm any other child or adult facelings. Many roaming child facelings usually walk around in sections, though rarely, they can wander inside main areas. Some wanderers have experienced hallucinations due to food consumption, hallucinating many unsafe entities, causing wanderers to stress, the only entities present in the level are facelings, do not believe any other entity. =='''Thunderstorms'''== Thunderstorms are audible events than can occur anytime throughout the level. Once the event begins, decently quiet precipitation noises will be heard outside the library with occasional time periods of thunder being heard. Pictures outside the windows do not change throughout the thunderstorm event. Thunderstorms often cause those capable of hearing it to gradually become calmer, and slower, with some cases stating the thunderstorm ambience possesses the ability to cause beings to take short naps. There is no definite time for how long a thunderstorm lasts, though wanderers have experienced storms that last for a minimum of 20 minutes. =='''Colonies and Outposts'''== There is 1 singular colony in level 356 that has been established. ==='''B.T.H.'''=== B.T.H., the acronym for 'Bright Tertiary Healers,' is a colony that has been established the day the level was discovered. The colony can allow more members up to 40. The colony's main purpose is to heal wanderers during mainly the aftermath of difficult situations, whether it means being attacked by a faceling or experiencing eye strain. The colony gives out resources and equipment that may be useful in other levels, like food and flashlights by sending some members out for expedition to get necessities during the day. In nighttime, members take turns scaring away child facelings to protect their base and awaken their base whenever morning arrives. The colony allows any wanderer free to join, unless it is at the limit of 40 members. {{Blurimage|align=Center|file=356BT-0.1.jpg|caption=Where the base usually groups at.}} =='''Entrances and Exits'''== === '''Entrances''' === * Zoning out in [[Level 208]] has a chance to send one to level 356. * Finding a mainly blue room inside [[Level 833]] and walking through it sends one to this level. * There is a chance that tripping over a Christmas light, intentionally or accidentally, in [[Level 941]] will send wanderers to Level 356. ==='''Exits'''=== * Sitting down on any chair during a thunderstorm event can send wanderers to three levels depending on the chair's color. Blue chairs lead to [[Level 161]]. Green chairs lead to [[Level 570]]. Orange chairs lead to [[Level 85|Level 85.]] Any other color chair will not send people anywhere. * [[Category:Levels]] Eating any kind of peppermint in a food court sends people to [[Level 360]] * Finding and picking up a black book in detailed sections will lead those to [[Level 86]]
